codeReview.summary: Solid implementation of the batch pricing endpoint. The feature adds GET /prices with comprehensive parameter validation (parse_batch_params, _parse_tokens, _parse_amounts), parallel pricing via ypricemagic get_prices, per-token caching with correct cache-skip semantics for amount-based requests, and Prometheus metrics. All 15 expectedBehavior items are covered by tests (55 new tests). Code follows existing patterns (lazy imports, ParseError/ParseSuccess unions, manual response dicts, error format). Helper functions (_resolve_batch_block, _prepare_batch_cache_check, _fill_batch_results) keep complexity manageable. The decomposition mirrors the single /price endpoint patterns closely.

issues.description: Dead code in fetch_amounts filtering: `if params.amounts[i] is not None` can never be False because params.amounts is tuple[float, ...] (never contains None). The subsequent `if len(fetch_amounts) != len(indices_to_fetch): fetch_amounts = None` guard is also unreachable. When amounts are provided, all tokens skip cache, so indices_to_fetch == range(len(tokens)) and fetch_amounts == params.amounts. Simplify to `fetch_amounts = params.amounts`.

_resolve_batch_block return type `int | tuple[int, JSONResponse]` uses a sentinel 0 as the first tuple element solely to distinguish error from success via isinstance check. This works but is an unusual pattern — a dedicated error class or raising an exception (as done in the /price endpoint) would be more idiomatic.

No defensive length check on get_prices result: if the upstream get_prices returns fewer elements than tokens (despite fail_to_None=True), the enumerate loop in _fetch_batch_prices would silently produce a shorter prices list, and _fill_batch_results would later IndexError. A guard like `assert len(results) == len(tokens)` or padding to expected length would make this more robust.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The batch /prices response elements omit 'chain' field that the single /price endpoint includes. This is per-spec but creates an inconsistency in API response shapes. The mission AGENTS.md documents the error format convention but not the response shape convention for different endpoints.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.py:395 - batch results have {token, block, price, block_timestamp, cached} vs src/server.py:508 single /price results have {chain, token, block, price, cached, block_timestamp}

sharedStateObservations.observation: New Prometheus metrics (batch_requests_total, batch_request_duration_seconds) were added but the architecture library doc only documents the original price_requests_total and price_request_duration_seconds metrics.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/server.py:43-51 defines batch metrics; .factory/library/architecture.md Prometheus section only lists price_requests_total and price_request_duration_seconds

summary: The batch-pricing-endpoint feature passes review. Implementation is complete and correct across params.py (BatchParams dataclass, parse_batch_params with token/amount/timestamp/boolean validation, max 100 token limit), server.py (GET /prices endpoint with cache-aware batching, parallel fetch via get_prices, partial-failure 200 semantics, Prometheus metrics), and comprehensive tests (55 new tests covering all 15 expectedBehavior items). Three non-blocking code quality issues noted: dead code in fetch_amounts filtering, unusual return type pattern in _resolve_batch_block, and missing defensive length check on get_prices result. Two shared state observations about undocumented metrics and response shape inconsistency.
